eGovERA Solution Architecture Framework

Appendices

Glossary of Terms

Term

Definition

Architecture Building Block (ABB)

Abstract, reusable element that defines required capabilities and interoperability characteristics without specifying implementation.

Architecture Implementation Method (AIM)

Method that structures architecture activities into phases aligned with analysis, design, and implementation lifecycle use cases.

Architecture Principle

A rule or guideline governing the design and use of IT resources and architecture decisions.

Cartography

Structured mapping of interoperability solutions and architecture elements to support reuse and decision-making.

Compliance

Verification that a solution adheres to legal, regulatory, and policy requirements.

Conformance

Verification that an architecture or solution correctly applies defined standards, building blocks, and modelling rules.

Digital Business Capability (DBC)

A capability required to support the delivery and operation of digital public services.

Digital Public Service (DPS)

A service delivered by a public administration to citizens, businesses, or other administrations using information and communication technologies.

Increment

A partial implementation that builds cumulatively on previous implementations.

Interoperability

The ability of systems and organisations to work together across legal, organisational, semantic, and technical dimensions.

Interoperability Test Bed

Platform for testing conformance of systems against semantic and technical specifications.

Intervention

A pilot activity focused on the definition and design of interoperable digital public services.

Legal Interoperability

Alignment of legislation, policies, and regulatory frameworks enabling digital public services to operate across jurisdictions.

Organisational Interoperability

Alignment of processes, roles, and governance structures to enable collaboration between organisations.

Reference Architecture (RA)

A standardised architectural framework used to guide the design and evaluation of systems.

Semantic Interoperability

Shared understanding of data through common vocabularies, data models, and definitions.

Solution Architecture Framework (SAF)

Framework that structures problem analysis and solution design within the architecture lifecycle.

Solution Building Block (SBB)

Concrete implementation of one or more ABBs using specific technologies, standards, or products.

Technical Interoperability

Compatibility of systems, applications, and infrastructure enabling communication and data exchange.

Tranche

A partial implementation that is largely independent of previous implementations within a lifecycle.

Validation

Assessment that a solution meets functional, non-functional, and interoperability requirements.

Table 1 Glossary of Terms

Abbreviations List

Abbr eviation

Term

Description

ABB

Architecture Building Block

Abstract, reusable element defining required capabilities.

AIM

Architecture Implementation Method

Method structuring architecture lifecycle phases and processes.

COTS

Commercial Off-The-Shelf

Proprietary software product used for implementation.

DBC

Digital Business Capability

Capability required to support digital public services.

DPS

Digital Public Service

Service delivered by public administrations using ICT.

eGovERA

eGovernment European Reference Architecture

Extension of EIRA providing more detailed and domain-specific architectures.

EIF

European Interoperability Framework

Framework for enabling interoperable digital public services across the EU.

EIRA

European Interoperability Reference Architecture

Reference architecture defining interoperability capabilities and building blocks.

ELAP

European Library of Architecture Principles

Repository of architecture principles supporting EIRA and eGovERA.

GITB

Generic Interoperability Test Bed

Standardised framework for interoperability testing.

IDS

Interoperability Data Service

Technical service supporting data exchange.

IMM

Interoperability Maturity Model

Tool for assessing interoperability maturity.

NIFO

National Interoperability Framework Observatory

Provides country insights and best practices.

OSS

Open-Source Software

Software with publicly available source code.

RA

Reference Architecture

A standardised architectural framework

SAF

Solution Architecture Framework

Framework guiding problem analysis and solution design.

SBB

Solution Building Block

Concrete implementation of one or more ABBs.

SOA

Service-Oriented Architecture

Architectural style based on loosely coupled services.

TDL

Test Description Language

Language used to define test cases in GITB.

Table 2 Abbreviations List

Conventions Used for Lifecycle Management Figures

Convention

Definition

A

Analysis Phase

D

Design Phase

T or τ

Each Tranche

Δ

Each Increment

L

Legal View

O

Organisational View

S

Semantic View

Ta

Technical – Application View

Ti

Technical – Infrastructure View

DBC

Digital Business Capability

DBCx

Corresponds a specific Digital Business Capabilities. x can be any number depicting DBC 1, 2, 3 ..

A(L)->A(O)

Defines the sequence to be followed, in this case, complete the Analysis of the Legal View before the Analysis of the Organisation view.

D(Ta( DBC1))->D(Ti(DBC1))

Defines the sequence to be followed, in this case, complete the Design of the Ta View for the DBC1 before the Design of the Ti for the DBC1.

Table 3 Lifecycle Figures Convention

Table 4 Useful Links

Joinup Courses

The following courses are available on the EU Academy platform:

Name

Description

Link

Introduction to EIRA

Discover how the European Interoperability Reference Architecture helps public administrations analyse and design interoperable digital public services across Europe.

Link

Introduction to eGovERA©

Learn how eGovERA supports the full digital public service lifecycle with practical architectural guidance, standards, and reusable building blocks aligned with EU policy.

Link

EIRA© & eGovERA© in Support to a Digital Public Service Analysis

Master the analysis of digital public services by identifying legal, organisational, semantic, and technical requirements.

Link

EIRA© & eGovERA© in Support to a Digital Public Service Design

Turn analysis into action by designing interoperable digital public services and ensuring traceability from requirements to implementation.

Link
